acc.advanced: Advanced parameters (only edited values are sent)
acc.api_usage: 🔌 API usage: how third-party apps generate speech with this server
acc.download_settings: 🔐 Download settings: HF token / proxy (optional, not saved)
analyze.no_results: (The model returned no displayable analysis results — no speech may have been detected in the audio.)
api.usage_md:
Third-party applications can call the `audiocpp_server` this WebUI starts directly (an OpenAI-style HTTP API), bypassing this page.

- **Which URL**: the API address is `{server}`; for OpenAI-compatible clients set the Base URL to `{server}/v1`.
  Speech: `POST {server}/v1/audio/speech` · Transcription: `POST {server}/v1/audio/transcriptions`
- **Which model name**: `model` takes the model id (the same ids as the model list on this page, e.g. `qwen3-tts`, `vibevoice`), and it must be the one **currently loaded** — the server holds only one model at a time, so click '{load}' on this page first. `GET {server}/v1/models` lists the ids currently available.
- **TTS request example** (the response is WAV audio by default; add `"response_format": "json"` to get base64 in JSON instead):

```bash
curl {server}/v1/audio/speech -H "Content-Type: application/json" -o out.wav \\
  -d '{{"model": "qwen3-tts", "input": "Hello from audio.cpp.", "voice_ref": "/path/to/voices/ref.wav", "reference_text": "the words spoken in the reference audio", "seed": 1234}}'
```

- **ASR request example**: `-d '{{"model": "qwen3-asr", "audio": "/path/to/audio/in.wav"}}'`.
  Optional `"language"` (force a language, e.g. `"Chinese"`) and `"context"` (a bias hint for names/terms) apply to qwen3-asr only.
  Note that `voice_ref` / `audio` are **file paths on the machine running the server**, not browser uploads.
- **Music generation (gen models)**: use the generic route `POST {server}/v1/tasks/run`, with a body like
  `{{"model": "ace-step", "request": {{"text": "prompt", "lyrics": "lyrics", "duration_seconds": 30,
  "options": {{"tags": "pop,bright"}}}}}}`. The response JSON's `audio` field is a base64 WAV.
- **Other tasks (vc/svc/s2s/sep/vad/diar/align)**: also `POST {server}/v1/tasks/run`, using fields such as
  `audio` (source audio path) / `voice_ref` (target voice) / `text` (alignment text) inside `request`. Separated stems come back in
  `named_audio_outputs`, and VAD/diarization/alignment results in `segments` / `speaker_turns` / `words`.
- The server's lifetime follows this WebUI's **terminal window**: closing only the browser tab changes nothing and the server stays callable by third parties; closing the terminal (webui.py exiting) shuts it down too. You can also start the server on its own (e.g. {launcher}) for third-party applications.
err.adv_json_invalid: Advanced parameters are not valid JSON: {error}
err.adv_json_not_object: Advanced parameters must be a JSON object, e.g. {"num_inference_steps": 10}
err.align_needs_transcript: Forced alignment needs the words spoken in the audio, in 'Alignment text'
err.analysis_ace_only: Only ACE-Step supports source audio analysis; select ACE-Step in the model list first
err.analysis_not_json: The analysis result from the server is not JSON: {raw}
err.audio_duration_note: ⏱ This audio is about {secs} seconds long
err.dialogue_needs_diar: Dialogue mode needs a diarization model: install Sortformer diar from the '{tab}' tab and retry.
err.dialogue_needs_pcm: Dialogue mode needs a readable PCM WAV (for splitting); this file cannot be read: {error}
err.diar_too_long: Diarization handles about {max_sec} seconds per run (Sortformer's fixed-graph/positional-encoding ceiling); shorten the audio first.
err.external_server_port: A server not started by this WebUI is holding {host}:{port} (for example {launcher}). Stop it, or point AUDIOCPP_SERVER at it.
err.external_server_session: The server on {host}:{port} was started outside this WebUI, so its session config cannot be adjusted on demand (this needs {options}). Stop it first.
err.ffmpeg_missing: ffmpeg not found (bundle it in the webui folder or install it on PATH); cannot transcode non-WAV audio.
err.lang_unsupported: The selected model does not support the language "{language}". Choose one of: {supported}, or leave it blank to use the model default.
err.model_id_unknown: no such model id in the catalog: {model_id}
err.model_manager_missing: ❌ tools/model_manager.py not found (point AUDIOCPP_MODEL_MANAGER at it)
err.model_not_installed: model not installed (directory does not exist): {path}
err.model_wrong_task: model {model_id} has task {task}, but this tab needs {expected}
err.no_audio_in_response: The server response contains no audio data
err.no_audio_in_response_gen: The server response contains no audio data (this model may not output audio)
err.no_prompt: Enter a prompt (what kind of music/sound effect do you want?)
err.no_source_to_convert: Upload the source audio to convert
err.no_speaker_turns: No speaker turns detected — the audio may contain no speech.
err.no_stems_in_response: The server response contains no stem data
err.server_exe_missing: server not found: {path} (choose one with AUDIOCPP_BACKEND=gpu|cpu)
err.server_unreachable:
Cannot reach server @ {server}: {error}
💡 The server may have exited; click '{load}' again.
err.turns_too_short: All speaker turns are too short to transcribe.
err.vdes_needs_description: Fill in 'Voice description' — a voice design model uses it to decide what the voice sounds like
err.vibevoice_text_too_short: VibeVoice is a long-form model: very short text comes out as gibberish throughout (a model trait — no parameter fixes it). Extend the text to ≥35 words (≥40 characters for CJK), or switch to a model that is stable on short input such as qwen3-tts / voxcpm2 / pocket-tts.
field.caption: Description
footer: audio.cpp WebUI · loads on demand, only one model resident at a time · see webui/README.md for details
hint.ace_step: **ACE-Step** music generation/editing: describe style, instruments, and mood in the prompt (English works best); lyrics are optional. Editing routes need a source audio — click '🔍 Analyze source audio' first. See webui/README.md for parameter details.
hint.chatterbox: **Chatterbox** voice cloning: a reference voice is required; languages are limited to English/Spanish/French/German/Italian/Portuguese/Korean (**no Chinese**). Leave blank for English.
hint.err.asr_too_long: ⏱ Audio too long: the Qwen3-ASR encoder caps at 1500 tokens (~13 tokens/second), so roughly 115 seconds per request. Shorten or split the audio before transcribing.
hint.err.chatterbox_lang: 🌐 Chatterbox only supports en/es/fr/de/it/pt/ko (no Chinese/Japanese/Russian, and no autodetect). Pick a supported language, or leave it blank for the default (English).
hint.err.graph_capacity: 📏 The audio exceeds the model's fixed graph capacity (Sortformer defaults to 20 seconds, with a ~120 second ceiling). The webui reloads diarization/dialogue mode to fit the duration automatically; if it still fails you are past the 120 second ceiling, so shorten the audio first.
hint.err.needs_reference_text: 🗒 Reference text is required: put the words spoken in the reference audio into 'Reference text'.
hint.err.needs_speaker_script: 📝 A multi-speaker script is required: write one `Speaker 0: text` per line (use Speaker 0/1/… for multiple roles).
hint.err.needs_voice_file: 🎤 A reference audio file is required (not a voice id): upload or record a reference voice.
hint.err.needs_voice_ref: 🎤 This model needs a reference voice: upload or record one, or pick a built-in reference voice, and retry.
hint.err.oom: 🧠 Out of VRAM: this request's compute graph does not fit in the remaining VRAM, usually because the audio or text is too long. Shorten it or split it into chunks and retry.
hint.err.stable_audio_english: 🎵 Stable Audio prompts are English only. Rewrite the prompt in English and retry.
hint.err.voice_samples_conflict: 🔀 voice_samples and a single reference voice cannot be used together: do not upload a reference voice for multi-speaker input.
hint.err.wav_only: 🎵 The server only accepts WAV audio. The webui transcodes uploads automatically, but options where you type a path yourself (such as voice_samples) must already point at a .wav.
hint.heartmula: **HeartMuLa**: the `tags` advanced option is required (comma-separated), and 'Lyrics' holds the sung words; peak VRAM ~25G, so an 8G card cannot run it.
hint.htdemucs: **HTDemucs**: outputs four stems — drums / bass / other / vocals.
hint.marblenet_vad: **MarbleNet VAD**: frame-level voice activity detection, outputs a list of speech segments.
hint.mel_band_roformer: **Mel-Band RoFormer**: outputs a vocals stem and an accompaniment stem.
hint.miocodec: **MioCodec**: codec resynthesis conversion — the source supplies the content, the reference supplies the timbre.
hint.pocket_tts: **PocketTTS**: a reference voice is required (upload, record, or built-in).
hint.qwen3_asr: **Qwen3-ASR**: long audio is transcribed in automatic chunks; see 'Transcription options' for language, context, and dialogue mode.
hint.qwen3_forced_aligner: **Qwen3 forced alignment**: put the audio's transcript in 'Alignment text' to get per-word timestamps (≤115 seconds).
hint.qwen3_tts: **Qwen3-TTS** voice cloning: uploading a reference voice and filling in 'Reference text' is recommended, otherwise output may be truncated early.
hint.seed_vc: **Seed-VC**: source speech + target voice reference (a few seconds of clean vocals); uses the v2 route by default, switch to the older v1 route in the advanced options.
hint.silero_vad: **Silero VAD**: detects speech segments in audio.
hint.stable_audio: **Stable Audio**: prompts are **English only**, and lyrics are unused; upload a source audio for init/inpaint (pick `audio_input_kind` in the advanced options).
hint.vevo2: **Vevo2**: source speech + target voice, converting only the timbre by default (speaking style is preserved). Style-conversion routes need `style_ref` and friends added via JSON — see webui/README.md. Long audio is chunked automatically and the reference voice is trimmed to ≤10s.
hint.vibevoice: **VibeVoice** multi-speaker script: one `Speaker N: text` per line (plain text is wrapped automatically); for multiple voices use the `voice_samples` advanced option instead of a reference audio.
hint.voxcpm2: **VoxCPM2** voice cloning: upload a clean single-speaker reference voice and fill in 'Reference text'; long text is chunked automatically.
label.align_text: Alignment text (required for align: the audio's transcript)
label.dialogue_mode: 🗣 Dialogue mode (≤120s, needs Sortformer): output a transcript with speakers and timestamps
label.extra_stems: , the remaining {n} stems are in the download list
label.voice_description: Voice description (describe the voice you want in words)
log.asr_chunking: {tag} long-audio chunking: {dur} → {n} chunks (≤{max_s}s each, split at silence)
log.dialogue_diar_first: dialogue mode: running diarization with {diar} first (audio {dur}s)
log.dialogue_done: dialogue transcription done: {turns} turns, {speakers} speakers, took {secs}s
log.diar_done: diarization done: {turns} turns → merged into {merged}; switching back to {model} to transcribe each
log.gen_start: music generation start: model={model}, target duration {dur}s
log.model_load_failed: model {label} failed to load / timed out ({timeout}s)
log.model_unloaded: unloaded model {label} and stopped the server; VRAM released
log.repeated_so_far: previous line repeated {n} times so far
log.repeated_total: previous line repeated {n} times in total
log.sep_start: source separation start: model={model}, audio duration {dur}
log.tts_failed_unreachable: TTS failed: chunk {i}/{n} cannot reach server
log.tts_start: TTS start: model={model}, {chunks} chunk(s) / {chars} chars total
log.vc_seg_note: , reference {ref}s, adaptively split into {n} chunks (≤{cap}s each)
log.vc_start: voice conversion start: model={model}, source audio {dur}{seg}
log.vdes_failed_unreachable: voice design failed: cannot reach server
msg.auto_16k_mono: *Input is converted to 16 kHz mono automatically.*
msg.dialogue_done: ✅ Dialogue transcription complete (audio {dur}s, {turns} turns, {speakers} speakers), took {secs}s.
msg.dialogue_no_text: ⚠️ Diarization found speaker turns, but none produced any text.
msg.dl_already_installed: ✅ {label} is already installed; no download needed
msg.dl_already_running: ⏳ {label} is already downloading in the background…
msg.dl_done: ✅ {label} finished downloading; click '{refresh}' next to it to refresh the available list.
msg.dl_no_download_id: ⚠️ {label} has no download_id configured (a bundled asset/dependency); install it manually
msg.dl_not_started: ⚪ {label} is not installed and no download has started
msg.no_advanced_params: *This model has no tunable advanced parameters.*
msg.server_not_running: ⚪ server not running @ {server} — pick a model and click '{load}'
msg.src_analysis_done: ✅ Analysis complete (audio {dur}), took {secs}s, and the results were written back into 'Advanced parameters' (editable). Check the lyrics before doing a remix/cover.
msg.unload_external: ⚠️ The running server was not started by this WebUI (e.g. {launcher}); stop it from the window that launched it.
msg.unload_not_running: ⚪ The server is not running; nothing to release.
msg.unloaded: 🧹 Model unloaded and VRAM released — the next 'Load Model' click, or simply generating/transcribing, reloads it automatically (~5s).
ph.analyze_output: Speech segments / speakers / per-word timestamps will appear here…
ph.context_hint: Names, terminology, etc. — helps recognize proper nouns
ph.hf_token: hf_xxx — or run huggingface-cli login first
ph.reference_text: The words spoken in the reference audio (recommended for cloning — the more accurate, the better)
ph.voice_description: e.g. a deep, resonant middle-aged male voice, slow-paced, with a broadcaster's tone
sec.gen_source: #### 🎧 Source audio (optional, for editing routes only)
sec.vc_target: #### 🎧 Target voice (whose voice to convert into)
value.tts_sample_text: Hello, this is audio dot cpp speaking from a web page.
value.vdes_sample_text: Hello — this voice was designed from a text description by audio.cpp.
warn.no_hf_token: ⚠️ No HF token detected — gated/restricted models will return 401. Enter a token above (or run `huggingface-cli login` first); gated models also require accepting their terms on their HF page.
warn.vram_before_download: ⚠️ **Not enough VRAM**: this model is estimated to need **≥{need}G**, and this machine has only **{have}G** — after downloading it will most likely spill into shared memory and be very slow, or fail to run at all.
warn.vram_hint: ⚠️ **VRAM note**: at default settings this model is estimated to need **≥{need}G** of VRAM, and this machine has **{have}G** — it will load but will spill into shared memory and run slowly.
warn.vram_hint_uninstalled: ⚠️ **VRAM note**: at default settings this model is estimated to need **≥{need}G** of VRAM, and this machine has **{have}G** — it will load but will spill into shared memory and run slowly, so think twice before downloading it.
